content.text: now noo need for search option/feature on blog page because of cmd+k

input.prompt:
Search the codebase to find:
1. The blog page component/file(s)
2. Any search functionality on the blog page (search input, search bar, search icon, etc.)
3. Related search components or utilities used by the blog

Look for files like blog.tsx, blog.jsx, page.tsx in a blog directory, and any search-related components. Return the exact file paths, line numbers, and relevant code snippets for:
- The blog page file(s)
- Any search UI elements on the blog page
- Import statements related to search on the blog page

result.output:
Here are my findings, organized by each of your three questions. --- ## 1. Blog Page Component/File(s) ### Server-side entry point **File:** `/home/himmel/personal/www/app/blog/page.tsx` This is the Next.js page component for `/blog`. It fetches all posts server-side and delegates rendering to the client component. - **Line 2:** `import { getAllPosts } from "@/lib/blog"` -- fetches posts from the filesystem - **Line 3:** `import { BlogIndexClient } from "@/components/blog-index-client"` -- renders the interactive UI - **Lines 23-26:** The `BlogIndex` default export function that calls `getAllPosts()` and passes the result to `` ### Client-side blog index component **File:** `/home/himmel/personal/www/components/blog-index-client.tsx` This is the main interactive blog listing component with all the search UI (detailed below). It renders: - A "Blog" heading (line 54) - A desktop search bar (lines 58-70) - A posts list (lines 73-112) - A mobile floating search button (lines 117-124) - A mobile search drawer (lines 127-133) ### Blog data utility **File:** `/home/himmel/personal/www/lib/blog.ts` Server-side utility that reads `.mdx`/`.md` files from `content/blog/`, parses frontmatter, calculates reading times, and exports types like `PostWithMeta`. --- ## 2. Search Functionality on the Blog Page There are **two search UI implementations** in the blog page -- one for desktop and one for mobile -- plus a global command menu search. ### A. Desktop Search Bar **File:** `/home/himmel/personal/www/components/blog-index-client.tsx`, lines 58-70 ```tsx {/* Desktop Search Bar */}
setQuery(e.target.value)} className="w-full border-0 border-b border-border bg-transparent pl-6 pr-6 py-2 text-sm placeholder:text-muted-foreground focus:border-foreground focus:outline-none focus:ring-0" />
``` - Uses `@tabler/icons-react` `IconSearch` for the search icon (line 6) - Controlled input bound to `query` state (line 30) - Hidden on mobile (`hidden md:block`), visible on desktop - Below the border-bottom style search input ### B. Mobile Floating Search Button **File:** `/home/himmel/personal/www/components/blog-index-client.tsx`, lines 117-124 ```tsx {/* Mobile Floating Search Button */} ``` - Only visible on mobile (`md:hidden`) - A floating circular button at the bottom center of the screen - Opens the `MobileSearchDrawer` by setting `isMobileSearchOpen` to `true` ### C. Mobile Search Drawer **File:** `/home/himmel/personal/www/components/mobile-search-drawer.tsx` (all 56 lines) A bottom-sheet drawer that slides up on mobile containing: - A search input with `IconSearch` icon (lines 36-45) - Result count display (lines 47-51) - A backdrop overlay for dismissal (lines 30-33) - Locks body scroll when open (lines 15-24) ### D. Search Debounce Logic **File:** `/home/himmel/personal/www/components/blog-index-client.tsx`, lines 30-47 ```tsx const [query, setQuery] = useState('') const [isMobileSearchOpen, setIsMobileSearchOpen] = useState(false) const [searchResults, setSearchResults] = useState([]) const [isSearching, setIsSearching] = useState(false) useEffect(() => { const doSearch = async () => { setIsSearching(true) const results = await searchPosts(query, posts, 20) setSearchResults(results) setIsSearching(false) } const timeoutId = setTimeout(doSearch, 100) return () => clearTimeout(timeoutId) }, [query, posts]) const displayedPosts: PostWithMeta[] = query.trim() ? searchResults : posts ``` - 100ms debounce on the search query - When `query` is empty, displays all posts; otherwise displays search results --- ## 3. Search-Related Imports and Utilities Used by the Blog ### Import statements in the blog client component **File:** `/home/himmel/personal/www/components/blog-index-client.tsx` | Line | Import | |------|--------| | 6 | `import { IconSearch } from '@tabler/icons-react'` | | 7 | `import { MobileSearchDrawer } from '@/components/mobile-search-drawer'` | | 9 | `import { searchPosts } from '@/lib/search'` | ### Search utility (Orama-based full-text search) **File:** `/home/himmel/personal/www/lib/search.ts` - Uses `@orama/orama` for in-memory full-text search (line 1) - **`initializeSearch(posts)`** (lines 55-85): Creates and populates an Orama search index with post data (slug, title, description, content, date, readingTime, tags). Caches the index and only rebuilds if posts change. - **`searchPosts(query, posts, limit)`** (lines 87-114): The main exported function called by the blog page. Initializes the index, runs the search, and maps results back to `PostWithMeta[]`. ### Global Command Menu (site-wide search including blog posts) **File:** `/home/himmel/personal/www/components/command-menu.tsx` - Uses `cmdk` library (line 5: `import { Command } from "cmdk"`) for a Cmd+K search palette - Lines 81-101: Lists all blog posts as searchable items, navigating to `/blog/${post.slug}` on selection - This is a site-wide search, not specific to the blog page, but it includes blog posts as search results ### SearchHint component (separate from blog page) **File:** `/home/himmel/personal/www/components/search-hint.tsx` - A standalone component that triggers the Cmd+K command menu by dispatching a synthetic `keydown` event - Has both mobile (icon-only button) and desktop (button with `Cmd+K` label) variants - Not directly used on the blog page, but available as a reusable search trigger component --- ## Summary of All Relevant File Paths | File | Purpose | |------|---------| | `/home/himmel/personal/www/app/blog/page.tsx` | Blog page (server component) | | `/home/himmel/personal/www/components/blog-index-client.tsx` | Blog page client component with search UI | | `/home/himmel/personal/www/components/mobile-search-drawer.tsx` | Mobile search drawer for blog | | `/home/himmel/personal/www/lib/search.ts` | Orama-based full-text search utility | | `/home/himmel/personal/www/lib/blog.ts` | Blog data fetching and types | | `/home/himmel/personal/www/components/command-menu.tsx` | Global Cmd+K search (includes blog posts) | | `/home/himmel/personal/www/components/search-hint.tsx` | Reusable search trigger button |

<path>/home/himmel/personal/www/lib/search.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: import { create, insert, search } from '@orama/orama'
2: import type { PostWithMeta } from './blog'
3: 
4: export type { PostWithMeta }
5: 
6: export type PostSearchResult = {
7:     id: string
8:     slug: string
9:     title: string
10:     description: string
11:     date?: string
12:     readingTime: number
13:     tags?: string[]
14: }
15: 
16: export type DisplayablePost = PostWithMeta
17: 
18: interface OramaDocument {
19:     slug: string
20:     title: string
21:     description: string
22:     content: string
23:     date: string
24:     readingTime: number
25:     tags: string
26: }
27: 
28: interface OramaSearchParams {
29:     term: string
30:     limit: number
31: }
32: 
33: interface OramaHit {
34:     document: OramaDocument
35: }
36: 
37: interface OramaSearchResult {
38:     hits: OramaHit[]
39: }
40: 
41: let searchIndex: ReturnType<typeof create> | null = null
42: 
43: const postSchema = {
44:     slug: 'string',
45:     title: 'string',
46:     description: 'string',
47:     content: 'string',
48:     date: 'string',
49:     readingTime: 'number',
50:     tags: 'string',
51: } as const
52: 
53: let cachedPosts: PostWithMeta[] | null = null
54: 
55: export async function initializeSearch(posts: PostWithMeta[]) {
56:     const postsKey = posts.map(p => p.slug).join(',')
57: 
58:     if (searchIndex && cachedPosts && cachedPosts.map(p => p.slug).join(',') === postsKey) {
59:         return searchIndex
60:     }
61: 
62:     searchIndex = create({
63:         schema: postSchema,
64:     })
65: 
66:     for (const post of posts) {
67:         const dateValue = post.frontmatter.date as unknown
68:         const dateStr = dateValue && typeof dateValue === 'object' && 'toISOString' in dateValue
69:             ? (dateValue as Date).toISOString()
70:             : String(dateValue || '')
71: 
72:         await insert(searchIndex, {
73:             slug: post.slug,
74:             title: post.frontmatter.title || '',
75:             description: post.frontmatter.description || '',
76:             content: post.content,
77:             date: dateStr,
78:             readingTime: post.readingTime,
79:             tags: post.frontmatter.tags?.join(' ') || '',
80:         })
81:     }
82: 
83:     cachedPosts = posts
84:     return searchIndex
85: }
86: 
87: export async function searchPosts(
88:     query: string,
89:     posts: PostWithMeta[],
90:     limit: number = 10
91: ): Promise<PostWithMeta[]> {
92:     if (!query.trim()) {
93:         return posts
94:     }
95: 
96:     const index = await initializeSearch(posts)
97: 
98:     const results: OramaSearchResult = await search(index, {
99:         term: query,
100:         limit,
101:     } as OramaSearchParams)
102: 
103:     return results.hits.map((hit: OramaHit): PostWithMeta => ({
104:         slug: hit.document.slug,
105:         frontmatter: {
106:             title: hit.document.title,
107:             description: hit.document.description,
108:             date: hit.document.date,
109:             tags: hit.document.tags?.split(' ').filter(Boolean),
110:         },
111:         content: '',
112:         readingTime: hit.document.readingTime,
113:     }))
114: }

(End of file - total 114 lines)
</content>

content.text: Now I'll remove the search feature from the blog page. Since `mobile-search-drawer.tsx` and `search.ts` are only used by the blog, I'll delete those too.

content.text:
Build compiled successfully - the error is a pre-existing GitHub API rate limit issue unrelated to my changes.

Here's what I did:
- **Simplified** `components/blog-index-client.tsx` — removed all search UI (desktop search bar, mobile floating button, mobile drawer), search state, and the debounce effect
- **Deleted** `components/mobile-search-drawer.tsx` (only used by blog)
- **Deleted** `lib/search.ts` (only used by blog; the Cmd+K command menu has its own search)
